surface: Android instrumentation on shared emulator emulator-5554 / AVD atermApi35 via :app:connectedDebugAndroidTest

isolation.sharedSurfaceBoundary: single Android instrumentation flow at a time

commandsRun.command: JAVA_HOME=/root/.local/share/aterm-jdk-17 ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/root/Android/Sdk PATH=/root/.local/share/aterm-jdk-17/bin:/root/Android/Sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin:/root/Android/Sdk/platform-tools:/root/Android/Sdk/emulator:$PATH /root/workspace/github/jtsang4/aterm/.factory/init.sh --check && adb -s emulator-5554 shell getprop sys.boot_completed && nc -z 127.0.0.1 3122
commandsRun.observation: Bootstrap check passed, emulator-5554 was boot-complete, and the repo-local SSH fixture was reachable on host port 3122.
A focused 9-test instrumentation suite passed and supplied the main proof for VAL-CROSS-002/003/004/006/007/008 plus partial coverage for VAL-CROSS-001.
An isolated rerun of the favorite repeat-use flow passed and supplied the proof for VAL-CROSS-005 and VAL-CROSS-009.

assertionsTested.title: A first-run password-identity setup can connect successfully and reconnect after app restart without re-entering setup data
observed: Password identities can be created through the UI and remain selectable after relaunch.
Password hosts can be saved and reopened after relaunch.
A seeded password-backed host can connect through the real app shell, keep trust state, and reconnect after relaunch.

evidence.consoleErrors: No app-specific failures were required for the partial UI proofs; instrumentation logcats only showed routine emulator/IME noise.
evidence.network: Partial real-session proof exists against 10.0.2.2:3122, but not as one end-to-end first-run setup flow.

assertionsTested.issues: No runnable proof chained first-run password-identity creation, host creation, first successful real connection, app restart, and reconnect in the same end-to-end user-surface flow. Existing tests only cover those pieces separately.

assertionsTested.title: An imported key identity can be used end to end to reach a real terminal session
observed: The flow imported a private key through the identity UI, created a key-auth host through the host UI, relaunched the app, and connected through the real app shell.
The host was explicitly switched into key auth mode and the imported key identity was selected before saving.
After connection, the test sent a terminal command and observed remote output proving a real terminal session on the SSH fixture without any visible password fallback path.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-specific app errors were reported in the copied logcat beyond routine emulator noise.
evidence.network: Real SSH connection from emulator to 10.0.2.2:3122 succeeded, followed by terminal output proof IMPORTED_KEY_SESSION_OK.

assertionsTested.title: A generated key identity can be used end to end to reach a real terminal session
observed: The flow generated a new key through the identity UI, created a key-auth host through the host UI, relaunched the app, and connected through the real app shell.
The generated key identity was selected from the reusable key identity chooser before host save.
After connection, the test sent a terminal command and observed remote output proving a real terminal session on the SSH fixture.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-specific app errors were reported in the copied logcat beyond routine emulator noise.
evidence.network: Real SSH connection from emulator to 10.0.2.2:3122 succeeded, followed by terminal output proof GENERATED_KEY_SESSION_OK.

assertionsTested.title: A trusted host remains trusted on later connections until the server key actually changes
observed: The first real-fixture connection presented a trust prompt, and later reconnects plus a post-relaunch reconnect succeeded without showing that prompt again.
The persisted trust state remained available after the linked identity metadata was edited and after app relaunch.
When the trusted key was intentionally changed, the app blocked the connection, and editing to a different endpoint required a fresh trust decision instead of silently reusing prior trust.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-specific app errors were reported in the copied logcats beyond routine emulator noise.
evidence.network: Real SSH connections to 10.0.2.2:3122 succeeded before trust reuse, and changed-key/endpoint-edit handling was exercised through the fixture trust flow.

assertionsTested.title: A snippet saved during normal use can be reused in a later terminal session on the intended host
observed: A saved-host snippet can be created through the snippets UI and persists as a saved snippet record tied to a chosen host.
In a later session, the repeat-use flow reopened the app, reconnected to the intended favorite host, opened snippets, and showed the saved-host target label before execution.
After confirmation, payload-specific remote output FAVORITE_REPEAT_SNIPPET_OK appeared in the terminal transcript and snippet history recorded the intended host label.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-specific app errors were reported in the copied logcats beyond routine emulator noise.
evidence.network: Real SSH reconnect and snippet dispatch to 10.0.2.2:3122 succeeded, with payload-specific transcript proof and saved-host history entry.

assertionsTested.title: Saved snippet-to-host association survives app relaunch and still targets the intended host
observed: A snippet can be saved against a chosen saved host in the snippets UI and is rediscoverable after relaunch with that saved-host target preserved.
After app relaunch in the port-edit flow, the snippet details still showed Saved host: Port edit host instead of degrading to active-session targeting.
Running the snippet after reconnect executed on the intended saved host and recorded history for that same saved host label.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-specific app errors were reported in the copied logcats beyond routine emulator noise.
evidence.network: Real SSH reconnect and saved-host snippet execution to 10.0.2.2:3122 succeeded after relaunch.

assertionsTested.title: Editing host or identity configuration propagates cleanly to both direct-connect and snippet-execution flows
observed: The saved host initially failed against the stale endpoint configuration.
After editing the host port and saving, a later direct reconnect succeeded against the updated fixture endpoint after relaunch.

The saved snippet targeting that host then executed successfully and recorded host-specific history, showing snippet execution also consumed the updated persisted configuration instead of stale cached state.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-specific app errors were reported in the copied logcat beyond routine emulator noise.
evidence.network: Initial connection to the stale endpoint failed, then direct connect and saved-host snippet execution both succeeded on the updated 10.0.2.2:3122 endpoint.

assertionsTested.title: Failed connection attempts do not destroy the saved local setup needed for retry
observed: After replacing the linked password with a wrong value, the app surfaced an authentication failure without corrupting the session transcript and left the saved identity repair path usable.
Re-entering the correct password through the identity UI allowed a successful retry, proving the saved host and identity setup survived the failed attempt.

In the separate stale-endpoint flow, the saved host and its saved-host snippet remained available through the failed connection, host correction, relaunch, and later successful direct connect plus snippet execution, with history still recording the repaired saved host.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-specific app errors were reported in the copied logcats beyond routine emulator noise.
evidence.network: Recoverable SSH failures were observed before later successful retries against 10.0.2.2:3122, and saved-host snippet execution still succeeded afterward.

assertionsTested.title: Repeat-use flows stay local-only and support fast reconnect from favorites or recents without any login or sync gate
observed: The flow relaunched the app, opened Hosts, selected the saved favorite host, and reconnected through the real app shell without recreating setup.
After reconnect, it opened Snippets, confirmed the saved-host target, and executed the saved snippet successfully in the later session.
The assertion explicitly checked that no Login, Sync, or Team text appeared in the critical path, so the repeat-use path stayed local-only.

evidence.consoleErrors: No assertion-specific app errors were reported in the copied logcat beyond routine emulator noise.
evidence.network: Real SSH reconnect and saved snippet execution to 10.0.2.2:3122 succeeded after reopening the app from the favorite path.

summary: Validated 9 assigned cross-area assertions on emulator-5554: 8 passed, 0 failed, 1 blocked (VAL-CROSS-001 lacked a single end-to-end first-run password setup proof).

frictions.description: A combined 10-test batch intermittently timed out on favorite_repeat_use_flow_reconnects_and_runs_saved_snippet_without_any_login_or_sync_gate when run late in a longer shared-emulator suite.
frictions.resolution: Reran that flow in isolation on the same emulator-5554 surface; the isolated rerun passed and its artifacts were used for VAL-CROSS-005 and VAL-CROSS-009.

frictions.description: The current Android instrumentation harness produced XML/textproto/logcat artifacts but no standalone per-step screenshots for these UI flows.

blockers.description: No existing runnable instrumentation proof covers the full first-run chain of creating a password identity, saving a host that uses it, connecting successfully through the real app shell, restarting the app, and reconnecting without re-entering setup data.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Combined the available UI identity, host, snippet, and real-session persistence tests, but they still only prove the pieces separately rather than as one end-to-end first-run flow.
